item.title: Tourists cool on Cyprus and Turkey as Iran war upends travel plans
item.description: Popular destinations in eastern Mediterranean suffer amid ‘generalised anxiety’

item.title: Chart of the Week: The oil shock hurts poorer economies hardest
item.description: Developing economies are more dependent on energy

item.title: Why the World Cup is heading to YouTube
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: NBA expansion, an Indian Premier League sale and more

item.title: A ‘victim’ and a ‘dinosaur’: three days of Crispin Odey testimony
item.description: Financier’s cross-examination this week has been culmination of his legal challenge against regulatory ban and fine

item.title: Egypt imposes emergency measures to save fuel
item.description: Prime minister says country’s natural gas imports bill has tripled since start of Iran war

item.title: Here’s the oil shock that Donald Trump can’t control
item.description: While there is a lot of spare crude on ships and in storage, stocks of refined products are much smaller

item.title: Lex in depth: Will the AI data centre boom become a $9tn bust?
item.description: The biggest groups splashing their cash may not make their money back, but will almost certainly live to tell the tale

item.title: Cash is still king this bonus season (can somebody tell the chancellor?)
item.description: Volatile markets show the vital role cash plays in investment Isa portfolios

item.title: Social media is populist and polarising; AI may be the opposite
item.description: Large language models elevate expert consensus and moderate views, in sharp contrast to social platforms

item.title: The HTSI spring property issue
item.description: Take the proactive approach, from a build-your-own Eames house to the TikTok realtors selling mega-homes on social media

item.title: Inheritance disputes surge to record levels as heirs fight for spoils
item.description: Rising asset values and increasingly complex families spark increase in cases heading to court

item.title: Thinking of installing a home battery? Here’s how
item.description: Two recent recruits to the ‘home energy revolution’ had very different experiences — both share their insights

item.title: Time to try cleaning products with skincare credentials?
item.description: Eco-friendly formulas, beauty industry-standard testing and subscription services are enticing converts to a new era of premium ‘clean’ products

item.title: KPMG to cut almost 600 UK jobs as slowdown persists
item.description: Big Four firm and rivals are struggling to regain footing even after reductions in headcount and costs

item.title: Ares appoints administrators to owner of French football club Lyon
item.description: Owner John Textor hits out at private capital firm’s ‘predatory’ decision to call in administrators

item.title: Memory chip stocks shed $100bn as AI-driven shortage trade unwinds
item.description: New research suggests AI data centres will need much less memory than investors had bargained for

item.title: UK diesel stockpiles at risk, warn traders
item.description: Commercial inventories could be used up by mid-May if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed

item.title: Stockpickers: Gamma Communications, Bellway, Kingfisher
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Google nears deal to help finance multibillion-dollar data centre leased to Anthropic
item.description: Texas site for Nexus Data Centers aims to avoid grid connection delays with direct gas supplies

item.title: US appeals court overturns $16bn Argentina ruling in blow to Burford Capital
item.description: Litigation funder’s share price drops 54% following decision by New York judges

item.title: US seeks to scrap offshore wind projects in exchange for fossil fuel deals
item.description: Interior department in talks with companies that hold leases to build plants off America’s coastline

item.title: Blackstone in advanced talks for aerospace parts maker Senior
item.description: Private capital group and industrial investor Tinicum in talks with FTSE 250 company

item.title: Saudi Arabia cancels building contracts for desert ski resort
item.description: Futuristic facility was part of kingdom’s $500bn Neom mega-project

item.title: Time to tackle the rank absurdities in UK energy policy
item.description: The debate over North Sea oil and gas resembles a bar brawl between opponents who can’t see straight

item.title: Lloyds faces £66mn car-finance lawsuit from 30,000 consumers
item.description: ‘Omnibus’ claim comes days before regulator unveils details of mis-selling redress scheme

item.title: BYD profits battered by China’s brutal EV price war
item.description: Tesla rival boosted by higher-margin exports as domestic competition intensifies

item.title: European jet fuel supplies under threat as Iran war halts flows
item.description: Warnings of supply crunch as imports drop and stocks dwindle

item.title: Trump administration blocked from punishing Anthropic over Pentagon row
item.description: Judge says classification of group as ‘supply-chain risk’ was probably ‘arbitrary and capricious’

item.title: Asda boss dismisses Reeves’ profiteering claim as ‘nonsense’
item.description: Allan Leighton rejects chancellor’s assertion that petrol retailers are price gouging as store chain’s profits sink

item.title: China retaliates against US trade probes ahead of Xi-Trump talks
item.description: Move marks escalation in tensions in run-up to much-anticipated May summit in Beijing
